results.rendered_html: 1. bandin m. (also written vandin, q.v., and m.c. ˚di) a praiser, bard, herald (who sings the praises of a prince in his presence or accompanies an army to chant martial songs; these bards are regarded as the descendants of a Kṣatriya by a Śūdra female),  Mn. ;  MBh.  &c.

bandin m.

GORR. 2,67,3 .  VIKR. 76 .  RAGH. 4,6 . 6,8 . 9,71 . 17,15 .  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 48,49 .  Spr. 909 . 3246 .  KATHĀS. 14,12. fgg.  71,69 . 122,73. fgg.  77 .  RĀJA-TAR. 5,351 .  PAÑCAT. 158,2 . bandimāgadhasūtaiḥ  MBH. 5,3144 . 15,624 .  HARIV. 5964 .  R. 1,9,13 . 2,26,12 .  R. GORR. 2,12,36 . 26,14 . 67,4 .  BHĀG. P. 10,70,20 . vidyādhara˚  KATHĀS. 44,141 . bandiputra = bandin  RAGH. 5,75 . nijavijñāna˚ ein Lobsänger seiner eigenen Künste  RĀJA-TAR. 3,141 . kusumaśarāsanaśāsanavandinī pikanikare  GĪT. 11,4 . bandī tu jāyate vaiśyānmāgadho vākyajīvanaḥ  MBH. 13,2573 . vandistrī  KULL. _zu_ M. 10,48 .
— 2〉 Gefangener, Sclave: surabandinaḥ  BHĀG. P. 11,4,15 . = surabhṛtyāḥ Comm. — Die  Bomb. Ausgg.  schreiben durchgängig bandin, aber vand, vandya u. s. w.
